description: For one claim to make another necessary: the first cannot be true, under the stated reading, while the second is false. Entailment is a relation between propositions alone, sharper than what caused an event or what a listener happens to find persuasive.
sound_symbolism: The grounded necessity of 'na' establishes a relation stronger than ordinary support. Deliberate 'the' asks the speaker to attend to scope and interpretation, while bright 'ri' carries the unavoidable consequence into focus.
grammatical_notes: A transitive verb with the entailing claim as subject and the entailed claim as object: 'ha mothare ha remuma natheri.' (This premise entails this conclusion.) The event-noun rule supplies entailment. Distinct from 'ka' (cause), 'thelao' (consequence in discourse), and 'panoru' (support), all of which may hold without necessity.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Precise entailment makes the conditions and interpretation of necessity visible rather than turning a forceful intuition into an absolute claim.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Participants can distinguish a necessary relation from persuasion, status, or insistence, reducing pressure disguised as logic.

tags.cognition: a necessary truth-preserving relation between propositions
